item.title: How Could Michelle Wilkins' Case Actually Happen?!
item.description:
In 2015, Michelle Wilkins, who was seven months pregnant, responded to a Craigslist ad for baby clothes in Longmont, Colorado. Police said she was lured to a home where she was attacked by Dynel Lane, who had been pretending to be pregnant.
Investigators said Lane assaulted Wilkins and removed her unborn baby in an attempt to present the child as her own. Wilkins survived and called 911, but the baby did not survive.
Lane was later convicted of attempted murder, assault, and unlawful termination of a pregnancy, and was sentenced to 100 years in prison. The case drew national attention due to how it was planned and carried out.

media:content.media:title: How Could Michelle Wilkins' Case Actually Happen?!
media:content.media:description:
In 2015, Michelle Wilkins, who was seven months pregnant, responded to a Craigslist ad for baby clothes in Longmont, Colorado. Police said she was lured to a home where she was attacked by Dynel Lane, who had been pretending to be pregnant.
Investigators said Lane assaulted Wilkins and removed her unborn baby in an attempt to present the child as her own. Wilkins survived and called 911, but the baby did not survive.
Lane was later convicted of attempted murder, assault, and unlawful termination of a pregnancy, and was sentenced to 100 years in prison. The case drew national attention due to how it was planned and carried out.

item.title: Harold Henthorn The Black Widower: The Chilling Murders Of Toni Bertolet & Lynn Henthorn
item.description: Harold Henthorn claimed to be a very unlucky man. His first wife, Lynn Rishell, died in a freak car accident. His second wife, Toni Bertolet, tragically fell from a cliff in Rocky Mountain National Park. But NPS investigators were convinced that their deaths were no accidents, and they started digging. That’s how Harold went from a successful, god-fearing businessman to Colorado’s infamous “Black Widower.”

media:content.media:title: Harold Henthorn The Black Widower: The Chilling Murders Of Toni Bertolet & Lynn Henthorn
media:content.media:description: Harold Henthorn claimed to be a very unlucky man. His first wife, Lynn Rishell, died in a freak car accident. His second wife, Toni Bertolet, tragically fell from a cliff in Rocky Mountain National Park. But NPS investigators were convinced that their deaths were no accidents, and they started digging. That’s how Harold went from a successful, god-fearing businessman to Colorado’s infamous “Black Widower.”

item.title: The Disturbing True Story Of Nicholas Barclay
item.description: 13-year-old Nicholas Barclay disappeared from his neighborhood in San Antonio, Texas, on June 13th, 1994. For three years, his family searched for their missing Nicky - until they got the shocking call that he was alive in Spain. For almost 5 months, the family believed the person staying in their home was their long lost son. But another shock came when it was revealed that this person was not Nicholas - it was 23-year-old French serial imposter Frédéric Bourdin. Frédéric admitted his lie - but he believes the family knows what really happened to Nicholas, who is still missing today.

media:content.media:title: The Disturbing True Story Of Nicholas Barclay
media:content.media:description: 13-year-old Nicholas Barclay disappeared from his neighborhood in San Antonio, Texas, on June 13th, 1994. For three years, his family searched for their missing Nicky - until they got the shocking call that he was alive in Spain. For almost 5 months, the family believed the person staying in their home was their long lost son. But another shock came when it was revealed that this person was not Nicholas - it was 23-year-old French serial imposter Frédéric Bourdin. Frédéric admitted his lie - but he believes the family knows what really happened to Nicholas, who is still missing today.
